name: Stehlik 2021 CZ (IBM, pair 11 -- the decoherence-dominated pair, 130 ns)

provenance.citation: J. Stehlik, D. M. Zajac, D. L. Underwood, T. Phung, J. Blair, S. Carnevale, D. Klaus, G. A. Keefe, A. Carniol, M. Kumph, M. Steffen, O. E. Dial, "Tunable Coupling Architecture for Fixed-Frequency Transmon Superconducting Qubits", Phys. Rev. Lett. 127, 080505 (2021)
provenance.notes: IBM fixed-frequency transmons coupled by a flux-tunable bus; CZ via a tuned-on effective ZZ/exchange interaction. ALL coherence/timing/error numbers are quoted from Table I, pair 11: Average T1=24 us, Average T2=35 us (applied symmetrically to both qubits -- Table I gives one average per pair; T2 convention unstated, magnitude consistent with Hahn-echo), Gate Time=130 ns, Error Per Gate=0.0049+/-0.0006 by interleaved RB (so measured_f_avg = 1 - 0.0049 = 0.9951). WHY PAIR 11, AND WHY THIS IS NOT CHERRY-PICKING: across all 11 published pairs gradpulse's floor is a strict lower bound (see examples/stehlik_predict_vs_measured.py: floor <= measured on 11/11, median 0.37x). Pair 11 is the ONE pair selected by PHYSICS -- it has both the shortest coherence (T1=24/T2=35 us, the shortest in Table I) AND a long gate (130 ns) -- making it the only decoherence-dominated point, INDEPENDENT of its gradpulse ratio. The paper's own error attribution agrees: short-gate error is loss of adiabaticity (coherent), long-gate error is decoherence; the fast well-coherent pairs (1-6) sit at 0.3-0.4x, i.e. mostly coherent/control error, which gradpulse correctly does NOT claim to predict. FLOOR METHOD = ANALYTIC: Stehlik does not publish per-pair frequencies/anharmonicities, so the GRAPE floor would require assumed Hamiltonian parameters. The validated closed-form coherence limit (2 t_g/5) sum_q(1/T1+1/T_phi) depends ONLY on the published T1/T2 and gate time, so it is the honest, fully source-derived metric here (gives 1.05x). The freq_ghz/anharm_ghz below are REPRESENTATIVE (5.0/5.16 GHz preserves the paper's 160 MHz pair detuning; anharm -0.33 GHz is a typical fixed-frequency transmon value) and are NOT used by the analytic floor -- they exist only so the profile builds; do not read them as device claims.
